definition: Volatility regime transitions follow a 'macro trigger, micro amplification' pattern where macro shocks initiate transitions and the ~$500B-$1.5T short-vol complex guarantees amplification through the financial accelerator mechanism (external finance premium convex in leverage) and microstructure channels (gamma hedging, VaR deleveraging, passive rebalancing).
evidence: Financial accelerator mechanism (Christiano-Motto-Rostagno 2014, Adrian and Shin 2014) is 'beyond serious dispute'
External finance premium is convex in leverage — amplification activates when intermediary balance sheets are stretched
Systematic short-vol strategies classified as Minsky Ponzi units whose forced liquidation sharpens transitions
Volmageddon (Feb 2018) provides small-scale proof of concept
Pair_2 debate: combined case that next transition will be amplified by short-vol complex rated 'persuasive'
open_questions: What is the magnitude of amplification relative to the underlying trigger?
AUM estimates for the short-vol complex span a 3x range ($500B-$1.5T) — what is the true figure?
Have dealer risk management improvements since 2018 reduced amplification potential?
